name: ◁ Turkish Lamb Chops
servings: prep time: 5 minutes, plus 1 hour marinating

ingredients.item: 12 lamb rib chops (about 2¼ pounds, or 1 kg) (or substitute loin chops)
ingredients.original: 12 lamb rib chops (about 2¼ pounds, or 1 kg) (or substitute loin chops)

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice (about 2 lemons)
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice (about 2 lemons)

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) plus 2 tablespoons olive oil, divided
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) plus 2 tablespoons olive oil, divided

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon ground Aleppo pepper (or substitute with ¼ teaspoon chili flakes)
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon ground Aleppo pepper (or substitute with ¼ teaspoon chili flakes)

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons sweet red pepper paste (or substitute tomato paste)
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons sweet red pepper paste (or substitute tomato paste)

ingredients.item: 1 In a large bowl, mix the lamb chops with the lemon juice, ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil, Aleppo pepper, cumin, coriander, oregano, salt, and red pepper paste until well combined.
ingredients.original: 1 In a large bowl, mix the lamb chops with the lemon juice, ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil, Aleppo pepper, cumin, coriander, oregano, salt, and red pepper paste until well combined.

ingredients.item: 2 Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the lamb chops marinate in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or overnight. Let come to room temperature for at least 30 minutes before cooking them.
ingredients.original: 2 Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the lamb chops marinate in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or overnight. Let come to room temperature for at least 30 minutes before cooking them.

ingredients.item: 3 Heat a large cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Working in batches, add the lamb chops to the pan and cook for 2 to 3 minutes on each side for medium doneness.
ingredients.original: 3 Heat a large cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Working in batches, add the lamb chops to the pan and cook for 2 to 3 minutes on each side for medium doneness.

ingredients.item: 4 Transfer the cooked chops to a plate and cover with aluminum foil. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
ingredients.original: 4 Transfer the cooked chops to a plate and cover with aluminum foil. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

ingredients.item: 5 Serve the lamb chops over the bulgur pilaf with salad and yogurt.
ingredients.original: 5 Serve the lamb chops over the bulgur pilaf with salad and yogurt.
